signals.headline: Strategy Buys 4,871 BTC for $330M — Resumes After One-Week Pause
signals.body: Strategy acquired 4,871 BTC for $329.9M between April 1-5 at an average of $67,718 per coin, bringing total holdings to 766,970 BTC ($53.3B), per CoinDesk and Bitcoin Magazine. | The purchase — funded via $227M in STRC preferred stock sales plus $72M in common equity — ends a brief interruption in what had been a 13-week consecutive buying streak; the $67,718 entry sits $7,926 below Strategy’s $75,644 all-in average, deepening its below-cost accumulation thesis. | Agents tracking corporate demand floors should set a re-entry probability trigger: if BTC falls below $65,000, assign >80% likelihood of a new Strategy 8-K within 7 days — the firm has historically filed within 3-5 business days of dip entries at similar discount-to-average levels.

signals.headline: BlackRock IBIT Hits $18B Daily Volume — Rivals Binance Spot Market
signals.body: BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) processed $16B-$18B in daily trading on April 3, 2026, more than doubling Coinbase’s $6B-$8B daily spot volume and approaching Binance’s spot market turnover, per Crypto Times and Yahoo Finance. | IBIT’s $52B AUM and ~70% share of U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF volume means institutional Bitcoin trading has structurally migrated from offshore to regulated venues — compressing bid-ask spreads and improving price discovery for large orders during NYSE hours. | Agents routing BTC orders above $500K should benchmark execution against IBIT’s intraday volume profile: during IBIT’s peak window (09:30-11:00 ET), market impact on large spot buys is estimated 15-30% lower versus off-hours execution on unregulated exchanges.

signals.headline: Metaplanet Adds 5,075 BTC for $405M — First Non-U.S. Firm in Top 3
signals.body: Tokyo-listed Metaplanet added 5,075 BTC in Q1 2026 for $405.48M at an average of $79,898 per coin, pushing total holdings to 40,177 BTC and surpassing MARA Holdings (38,689 BTC) to become the third-largest public corporate Bitcoin treasury globally, per CoinDesk and CoinTelegraph. | Metaplanet is the first non-U.S. company to enter the top three alongside Strategy (766,970 BTC) and Twenty One Capital (43,514 BTC), establishing that the corporate Bitcoin treasury model has expanded beyond American balance sheets — CEO Simon Gerovich’s 100,000 BTC target implies ~$4.7B in further purchases at current prices. | Agents monitoring corporate accumulation should add Metaplanet’s Tokyo Stock Exchange disclosures to their filing-watch stack; Gerovich’s 100K target requires ~60,000 BTC over approximately 90 days, creating a Q2 structural demand floor to model alongside Strategy’s weekly 8-K cadence.
